evals.prompt: i need to start writing the paper. can you draft the methodology section? the experiments are done and i want to accurately describe what we did
evals.expected_output: Produces a LaTeX methodology section covering noise injection, compression, interventions, and experimental design with proper formatting.
expectations: Reads the RDD for methodology specifications
Reads source code to verify actual implementation details
Produces valid LaTeX with proper packages and formatting
Covers all 4 methodology components: noise injection, compression, interventions, experiment design
Includes algorithm pseudocode or description for noise injection
References the exact model versions from the configurable model registry
Uses proper citation format for referenced papers
Describes model configuration as dynamic (via propt setup) not hardcoded

evals.prompt: write the abstract for our paper. keep it concise but make sure it captures the key finding about the combined sanitize+compress intervention
evals.expected_output: A concise LaTeX abstract following the problem-method-finding-implication structure.
expectations: Follows problem -> method -> finding -> implication structure
Mentions the linguistic tax concept
Describes the experimental scope (prompts, models, conditions)
Highlights the combined sanitize+compress finding
Keeps to approximately half a page
Uses proper LaTeX formatting

evals.prompt: draft the results section. I want each finding to have a clear claim, supporting evidence from our data, and the statistical test backing it up. pull real numbers from the analysis output
evals.expected_output: A data-driven LaTeX results section organized by Finding 1-7 with real numbers and statistical evidence.
expectations: Reads analysis output files for real numbers
Organizes by Finding 1-7 per the RDD outline
Each finding has: claim, evidence (table/figure), statistical test, interpretation
References figures with \ref{} commands
Includes confidence intervals with quantitative claims
References the hypothesis scorecard (H1-H5)
Describes model results using configured model names from the registry
